ksa_text: Knowledge of model inventory management practices including model tiering by materiality and complexity, risk classification, lifecycle tracking from development through retirement, and documentation standards.

dcwf.top_element_label: Develop and document supply chain risks for critical system elements, as appropriate.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information

top_anchors.element_label: Develop and document supply chain risks for critical system elements, as appropriate.
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to Model Risk Manager scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("risk classif", "model inventor"). STRM composite is moderate (0.4259); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working Model Risk Manager self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in Model Risk Manager work (domain affinity 0.90, 3 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.

decision_rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to Model Risk Manager scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("risk classif", "model inventor"). STRM composite is moderate (0.4259); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.
